ksa_text: Knowledge of AI regulatory frameworks and compliance requirements, including the EU AI Act risk classification structure, conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, high-risk system obligations, documentation requirements, technical documentation standards, and obligations on providers versus deployers.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: knowledge maps directly to AI Transparency and Documentation Specialist scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("risk classif", "conformity.{0,10}assess", "eu ai act"). STRM composite is strong (0.5243); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: External validation present: 1 match(es) in RSK job postings or certification scope. Competency appears in RSK role descriptions — sufficient to uphold.
